methods.description: Statistical procedures used to analyze the collected data, including the use of ANOVA to determine statistical significance in the measured properties of honey samples.

steps.description: All experiments were performed in triplicates, and the obtained quantitative results were expressed as the mean ± standard deviation.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate mean and standard deviation for triplicate experiments

steps.description: One-way analysis of variance (ANOVA) was performed, where a p-value of less than 0.05 was considered statistically significant.
steps.searchable_summary: perform one-way ANOVA with significance threshold of p < 0.05

methods.description: High Performance Thin Layer Chromatography (HPTLC) analysis of organic extracts from honey samples to obtain fingerprints with and without prior filtration.

steps.description: Approximately 1 g of each honey was dissolved in 2 mL of de-ionised water.
steps.searchable_summary: dissolve honey in de-ionised water

steps.description: Duplicate samples were prepared, and half the samples were filtered (0.45 μm) prior to extraction.
steps.searchable_summary: filter half of the honey solutions through 0.45 μm filter

steps.description: The extraction was carried out either with Dichloromethane (DCM) only or with a mixture of Acetonitrile (ACN):Dichloromethane (1:1; v/v).
steps.searchable_summary: extract honey solution with DCM or ACN:DCM mixture

steps.description: The organic solutions were dried with Na2SO4 anhydrous, and the resulting extracts following solvent evaporation were stored at 4°C.
steps.searchable_summary: dry organic solutions with Na2SO4 and store extracts at 4°C

steps.description: Prior to HPTLC fingerprinting, the extracts were reconstituted in 100 μL Dichloromethane (for DCM extracts) or Methanol (for ACN:DCM extracts).
steps.searchable_summary: reconstitute extracts in DCM or Methanol

steps.description: For the HPTLC analysis, glass plates were prepared by applying the organic honey extracts as 8 mm bands.
steps.searchable_summary: apply organic extracts to HPTLC plates as 8 mm bands

steps.description: Chromatographic separation was performed using a mixture of Toluene: Ethyl acetate: Formic acid as mobile phase.
steps.searchable_summary: perform chromatographic separation with specified mobile phase

steps.description: The obtained chromatographic results were documented using a HPTLC imaging device under 254 nm and 366 nm.
steps.searchable_summary: document chromatographic results with HPTLC imaging device

steps.description: The plate was then derivatised with Vanillin-Sulfuric acid reagent and heated for 3 min at 115°C.
steps.searchable_summary: derivatise plate with Vanillin-Sulfuric acid and heat

steps.description: The plate was cooled to room temperature and analysed again with the HPTLC imaging device under white light and at 366 nm.
steps.searchable_summary: analyse derivatised plate with HPTLC imaging device

methods.description: Detection and quantification of major sugars in honey samples using HPTLC, including preparation of standard solutions and chromatographic separation.

steps.description: Aqueous methanolic honey solutions (1 mg/mL) were prepared.
steps.searchable_summary: prepare aqueous methanolic honey solutions

steps.description: 2 μL of the solutions were applied as 8 mm bands on HPTLC plates.
steps.searchable_summary: apply honey solutions to HPTLC plates as 8 mm bands

steps.description: Standard solutions of glucose and fructose were also applied at various volumes.
steps.searchable_summary: apply standard glucose and fructose solutions to HPTLC plates

steps.description: Chromatographic separation was performed using 1-Butanol:2-Propanol:Boric acid as mobile phase.
steps.searchable_summary: perform chromatographic separation with specified mobile phase

steps.description: The plate was derivatised with Aniline-Diphenylamine-Phosphoric acid reagent and heated for 10 min at 115°C.
steps.searchable_summary: derivatise plate with Aniline-Diphenylamine-Phosphoric acid and heat

steps.description: The plate was cooled to room temperature and analysed with the HPTLC imaging device under transmission white light.
steps.searchable_summary: analyse derivatised plate with HPTLC imaging device

methods.method_name: HPTLC Analysis of Organic Honey Extracts
methods.description: HPTLC analysis to capture banding patterns of organic honey extracts, representing the honey's nectar source.

steps.description: HPTLC banding patterns, capturing Rf value, colour and intensity of each band, were obtained for a honey's organic extract at 254 nm and 366 nm prior to, as well as at 366 nm and white light after derivatisation.
steps.searchable_summary: capture HPTLC banding patterns at specified wavelengths

steps.description: Samples S1 -S11 present a very coherent banding pattern in the HPTLC analysis of their organic extracts, independent of the type of mobile phase used.
steps.searchable_summary: analyse coherence of HPTLC banding patterns across samples

steps.description: In samples S2 and S4, a bright blue band can be observed at white light after derivatisation, considered a marker band of Banksia sessilis nectar.
steps.searchable_summary: identify marker bands in HPTLC patterns

steps.description: A slight contribution of the pollen present in the samples can be noted when comparing the respective fingerprints prior and after filtration.
steps.searchable_summary: compare HPTLC fingerprints before and after filtration
